title: Headers and scope

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Headers and scope; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Headers and scope
sources.claim: th cells and scope associate simple row or column headers with data cells so values have context outside visual position.

sources.locator: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2.2; W3C Recommendation 2023; provisions applicable to Headers and scope
sources.claim: th cells and scope associate simple row or column headers with data cells so values have context outside visual position.

canonicalExplanation.summary: th cells and scope associate simple row or column headers with data cells so values have context outside visual position.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Each score column uses th scope=col and each learner name uses th scope=row. Your goal is to mark up and inspect header relationships for a simple and a multi-level table. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Heading hierarchy; Rows, cells and sections. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: th cells and scope associate simple row or column headers with data cells so values have context outside visual position. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Multi-level or irregular tables may need explicit id and headers associations, and scope must match actual structure. This draft therefore rejects: Bold text in the first row is enough for assistive technology to infer table headers.

includes: Associate row and column headers with data cells using the simplest sufficient mechanism.
Mark up and inspect header relationships for a simple and a multi-level table.
excludes: Multi-level or irregular tables may need explicit id and headers associations, and scope must match actual structure.

outcomes: Mark up and inspect header relationships for a simple and a multi-level table.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Use th with correct scope for simple tables and explicit associations when header relationships are complex.
